error_signature: secretOrPrivateKey must be an asymmetric key when using RS256
title: Salesforce JWT authentication error due to key input method
symptom: When testing Salesforce credentials in n8n, the error 'secretOrPrivateKey must be an asymmetric key when using RS256' is displayed. This occurs regardless of the hosting type or environment.
applies_when: Using Salesforce credentials with JWT authentication
not_this_if: Error occurs in a different context unrelated to key input
root_cause: The error arises when the private key is not correctly inputted into the n8n credentials. Users may mistakenly paste the key into the fixed input field instead of the expression editor, leading to improper formatting and authentication failures.
fix_steps: Open the Salesforce credentials in n8n
Ensure you are using the expression editor for the private key input
Paste the key value into the expression editor
Save the credentials and test again
agent_instruction: Use the expression editor to input the Salesforce private key correctly.
